Despite being scheduled and triggered manually, these jobs do not run, indicating a potential bug affecting isolated session job execution post-upgrade. The issue is documented on the openclaw repository, with steps to reproduce involving creating and manually running an isolated session cron job. The problem impacts users on macOS 15.7.4 using Node.js v22.22.0. The issue remains open, highlighting a need for a bug fix or performance fix in the upcoming releases.

members.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression introduced in openclaw version 2026.3.2, where isolated cron job sessions using the kimi-coding/k2p5 model can no longer access tools or the workspace. Despite previous functionality, these sessions are now unable to perform tasks such as reading files, which impacts automation workflows. The problem appears specific to Kimi models and persists across recent releases. To reproduce, users create a cron job that attempts to read a file in the workspace, which fails due to the tool access restriction. The issue is ongoing and affects automation and scripting capabilities within openclaw when using Kimi models.

representative.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the OpenClaw project related to OAuth profile expiry display. Previously, the system incorrectly showed stale expiry information ("expires in 0m") for auto-renewable OAuth profiles, despite the status being overridden to "ok" after a refresh. The fix involves clearing the `expiresAt` and `remainingMs` fields when profiles are set to auto-renew, ensuring that downstream renderers accurately reflect the profile's active status without displaying misleading expiry data. This update improves the correctness of OAuth profile status indicators, preventing confusion caused by outdated expiry information.

Notes: - Use `scripts/package-mac-dist.sh` for release artifacts (zip + DMG + notarization). Use `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` for local/dev packaging. - For `BUILD_CONFIG=release`, `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` now defaults to universal (`arm64 x86_64`) automatically. You can still override with `BUILD_ARCHS=arm64` or `BUILD_ARCHS=x86_64`. For local/dev builds (`BUILD_CONFIG=debug`), it defaults to the current architecture (`$(uname -m)`). - You can still override `APP_BUILD` explicitly when release engineering needs a specific monotonic value. - If `APP_BUILD` is omitted, `scripts/package-mac-app.sh` derives a Sparkle-safe default from `APP_VERSION` (`YYYYMMDDNN`: stable defaults to `90`, prereleases use a suffix-derived lane) and uses the higher of that value and  ...    ```bash

SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY_FILE=/path/to/ed25519-private-key scripts/make_appcast.sh dist/OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml ``` Generates HTML release notes from `CHANGELOG.md` (via `scripts/changelog-to-html.sh`) and embeds them in the appcast entry. Commit the updated `appcast.xml` alongside the release assets (zip + dSYM) when publishing. ## Publish & verify - On a previous public build, run “Check for Updates…” from the About tab and verify Sparkle installs the new build  ...    - `curl -I https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml` returns 200.
- Sanity checks:  ...    `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml`. - Upload `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.zip` (and `OpenClaw-2026.3.11.dSYM.zip`) to the GitHub release for tag `v2026.3.11`. members.summary: This pull request addresses a startup failure issue in the macOS version of openclaw. Previously, if the gateway startup failed (e.g., due to missing CLI), the onboarding wizard would still wait for a readiness timeout before displaying a generic message, leading to a poor user experience. The update modifies the `GatewayProcessManager.waitForGatewayReady()` function to now immediately return if the gateway status is `.failed`, allowing the onboarding process to fail fast and provide more accurate feedback. This fix improves error handling and responsiveness during macOS startup, enhancing overall stability and user experience.

members.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the OpenClaw project where, during high load conditions, the Feishu bot-info probe (`GET /open-apis/bot/v3/info`) can timeout after the hardcoded 10-second limit due to event-loop contention. This results in the `botOpenId` being empty, causing the `checkBotMentioned()` function to incorrectly return false for all group messages, effectively dropping them silently. The fix prevents these silent drops by handling the timeout more gracefully, ensuring reliable message processing in group chats. The update involves 73 lines of code changes across five files and aims to improve the robustness of the Feishu integration under load.
